basic_details.job_title: ICGEB Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: International Centre for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology (ICGEB), New Delhi
basic_details.total_vacancies: 02 (One for Lignin Valorization + One for Biomanufacturing)
basic_details.short_information: ICGEB New Delhi is recruiting Two Junior Research Fellows in the Microbial Engineering Group for cutting-edge research on lignin valorization and biomanufacturing. M.Sc/M.Tech holders in Biotechnology/Life Sciences with molecular biology experience can apply.

important_dates.interview_date: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted

age_limit.age_details: Initially 1 year, extendable annually based on performance and funds

qualification.details: criteria: Details | job responsibilities: Bacterial strain engineering, preparing technical reports, conducting experiments, and achieving project milestones
qualification.education_qualification: M.Sc/M.Tech in Biotechnology, Life Sciences or other related field with experience in enzymology/molecular biology techniques

vacancy_details.Job Focus: Converting lignin derived products into useful compounds using bacterial strain engineering
Molecular/Synthetic biology experiments for genome engineering of bacterial strains for commercial product production

post_wise_breakdown.job_focus: Converting lignin derived products into useful compounds using bacterial strain engineering
Molecular/Synthetic biology experiments for genome engineering of bacterial strains for commercial product production

selection_process: Selection will be based on scrutiny of applications followed by Interview. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. The cover letter must mention pertinent skills.
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should email their latest curriculum vitae and cover letter (Cover letter must mention pertinent skills) with a detailed description of scientific experience to [email protected]. Subject Line: “Application for JRF position in Microb Eng Group” Last Date: 26 July 2026
important_instructions: The positions are temporary and project-based. Salary will be as per DST norms. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interview. The position duration is only for the tenure of the sanctioned project.

content_sections.heading: ICGEB Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 Notification
content_sections.text: ICGEB New Delhi invites applications for Two JRF positions in the Microbial Engineering Group. The selected candidates will work on lignin valorization and biomanufacturing projects involving bacterial strain engineering, molecular biology, and metabolic engineering. The positions are temporary and project-based. Download ICGEB New Delhi JRF Official Vacancy Notice PDF

content_sections.heading: ICGEB New Delhi Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: ICGEB New Delhi is recruiting Two Junior Research Fellows in the Microbial Engineering Group for cutting-edge research on lignin valorization and biomanufacturing. M.Sc/M.Tech holders in Biotechnology/Life Sciences with molecular biology experience can apply.

content_sections.heading: ICGEB Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

rows.details: M.Sc/M.Tech in Biotechnology, Life Sciences or other related field with experience in enzymology/molecular biology techniques
GATE/NET qualification with experience in molecular biology, enzyme kinetics and/or genome engineering
Experience in Metabolic engineering and genome engineering
Bacterial strain engineering, preparing technical reports, conducting experiments, and achieving project milestones

content_sections.heading: ICGEB Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection will be based on scrutiny of applications followed by Interview. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. The cover letter must mention pertinent skills.

Subject Line: “Application for JRF position in Microb Eng Group” Last Date: 26 July 2026

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions for ICGEB Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 Candidates
content_sections.text: The positions are temporary and project-based. Salary will be as per DST norms. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interview. The position duration is only for the tenure of the sanctioned project.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for ICGEB New Delhi JRF positions 2026?
faq.answer: The closing date for applications is 26 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many JRF vacancies are available in the Microbial Engineering Group at ICGEB?
faq.answer: There are Two JRF positions (one for lignin valorization and one for biomanufacturing).

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for JRF at ICGEB New Delhi?
faq.answer: Salary will be as per DST norms, based on qualification and experience.

faq.question: Q4. What is the essential qualification for ICGEB JRF positions?
faq.answer: M.Sc/M.Tech in Biotechnology, Life Sciences or related field with experience in molecular biology techniques.

faq.question: Q5. What are the two JRF projects available at ICGEB Microbial Engineering Group?
faq.answer: Lignin valorization and Biomanufacturing.

faq.question: Q6. How to apply for ICGEB New Delhi Junior Research Fellow vacancy?
faq.answer: Email CV and cover letter (mentioning pertinent skills) to [email protected] with subject “Application for JRF position in Microb Eng Group”.

faq.question: Q7. What experience is desirable for the lignin valorization JRF position?
faq.answer: GATE/NET qualification with experience in molecular biology, enzyme kinetics and/or genome engineering.

faq.question: Q8. Is the JRF position at ICGEB New Delhi permanent?
faq.answer: No, it is a temporary project position initially for one year with possibility of extensions.

faq.question: Q9. Who is the lab head for the Microbial Engineering Group at ICGEB?
faq.answer: Dr. Shams Yazdani.

faq.question: Q10. What is the main focus of the biomanufacturing JRF position at ICGEB?
faq.answer: Molecular/Synthetic biology experiments to engineer bacterial strains for production of a commercial product.
